sources: World Bank WDI API (api.worldbank.org) — unemployment, GDP growth, services employment
Foundit/Naukri search data — city-wise job vacancies Engineering/MBA
BWPeople: Fresher hiring trends, Software/Hardware leads with 32% share
Business Standard/BusinessToday: TCS layoffs, hiring freeze 2025

macro.notes: World Bank 2024-2025 ILO estimates. India unemployment at post-COVID low of 4.2%. GDP growth robust at 6.49%. Macro environment supportive for placements.

Bengaluru.notes: GCC hub. AI/ML roles up 45-60% YoY. IT services headcount decline offset by 1,700+ GCCs. Avg fresher salary ₹4.16 LPA.

Mumbai.notes: BFSI-driven tech hiring. Fintech (Razorpay, PhonePe, Groww) actively hiring freshers.

Hyderabad.notes: Semiconductor boom (Micron, Qualcomm, AMD R&D). Pharma IT. 8% of India fresher postings.

Pune.notes: Automotive tech + IT services + manufacturing. 8% of India fresher postings.

Delhi NCR.notes: Top city: 21% of all India fresher job postings (Foundit data). GCC + startup hub.

Chennai.notes: Auto tech + IT manufacturing + IT services. Slower growth vs other hubs. 8% fresher postings.

Bengaluru.notes: 53,545 MBA job vacancies on Foundit (largest city). Startup + tech cos dominant.

Mumbai.notes: India's financial capital. BFSI + consulting hub. IIM Bangalore offers up 15.4% YoY. 682 MBA vacancies. Consulting avg ₹28-45 LPA.

Hyderabad.notes: BFSI + pharma + IT. Growing MBA demand driven by GCC expansion.

Pune.notes: Auto + manufacturing + IT. MBA placements improving, avg salary up 3-7% YoY.

Delhi NCR.notes: Consulting + FMCG + BFSI. XLRI, MDI Gurgaon, SPJIMR 100% placement records continue.

Chennai.notes: Manufacturing + auto + IT services. Moderate MBA demand compared to other metros.

Bengaluru.notes: Growing private hospital sector. Manipal, Apollo, Fortis expanding. Metro freshers earn ₹6-8 LPA.

Mumbai.notes: High demand. Top pay: up to ₹40,865/month experienced. Major hospital chains recruiting.

Hyderabad.notes: Corporate hospital boom: Apollo, Yashoda, KIMS expanding aggressively.

Pune.notes: Growing hospital sector. Tier-2 city salary benchmark ₹4.5-5.5 LPA.

Delhi NCR.notes: Highest nursing salary city. AIIMS + private chains. National shortage: 2.4M nurses needed.

Chennai.notes: Apollo headquarters. Medical tourism hub. Consistent demand for qualified nurses.

active_shocks.trigger: TCS hired ~40,000 freshers in FY2024-25 vs 110,000+ in FY2022-23 (-64%). TCS froze lateral hiring and paused salary hikes. Infosys delayed offer-letter joinings 12-18 months. Top-5 IT firms added only 4,787 net employees in Q1 FY2026.
active_shocks.recommended_action: Review underwriting for IT services freshers; verify actual joining date before disbursement; flag students with unconfirmed TCS/Infosys offer letters as HIGH risk

active_shocks.trigger: Byju's insolvency proceedings initiated 2024; 15,000+ layoffs cumulatively. Unacademy, Vedantu funding dried up. Several edtech unicorns have unpaid salaries.
active_shocks.recommended_action: Classify edtech employment as high-risk income source; require 6+ months salary credits; apply HIGH risk override for edtech company offer letters

positive_signals.description: India added 150+ new GCCs in FY2024-25, total 1,700+, employing 1.9M professionals. JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Apple, Nvidia, Walmart all expanded India headcount.
AI/ML roles demand up 45-60% YoY. Salary premium 30-50% over non-AI equivalents. 3:1 demand-to-supply ratio.
HDFC Bank, ICICI, Axis + fintech (Razorpay, PhonePe, Groww) continued healthy tech hiring. India job market growing 9% in 2025; IT sector up 15%.
India Semiconductor Mission: Micron $2.75B Sanand fab, Tata Electronics, Qualcomm/Intel/AMD R&D centres. Chip design roles up ~35% YoY.
India needs 2.4 million additional nurses. Government + private hospital chains expanding rapidly. Metro nursing salaries up 20-40% vs rural.

key_market_facts: IT job demand touched 1.8 million openings in 2025 (ZeeBiz)
Fresher hiring in India projected to surge 40% in FY2025-26 recovery
Top 4 IT firms (TCS, Infosys, HCLTech, Wipro) to onboard 82,000 graduates in FY2026
Delhi/NCR leads with 21% of fresher job postings; Bengaluru 14%
TCS FY2024-25 fresher intake: ~40,000 vs 110,000+ in FY2022-23 (-64%)
India's job market growing 9% in 2025, IT-software +15% (Foundit/Storyboard18)
2.4 million additional nurses needed in India
AI/ML roles: 45-60% demand growth, 30-50% salary premium
IIM Bangalore 2025: offers up 15.4% YoY, students placed up 16.4%
